rulings.Case Title: CALEF HOUSE, LLC, A BUSINESS ENTITY VS. FAY SERVICING, LLC, A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 5, 2021 line 1. OTHER HMC ASSETS, LLC, SOLELY AS ADMINISTRATOR FOR DEMURRER to 3RD Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, to support currently stated causes of action (no new causes of action may be added without leave of Court). Specifically, Plaintiff shall allege that the loan amount could not have been increased from the amount demanded in the NOD to the amount demanded in NOTS by any lawful charges other than monthly installment payments (e.g. default interest, late fees, attorney's fees, costs, insurance, taxes, etc; that amount at which the property was listed for sale and that offers were solicited/received to cover the proper outstanding amount of debt. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARK LAMBROS KURZENHAUSER VS. HELEN MARIE SCHOLTEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 5, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT HELEN SCHOLTEN, LISABETH FINCI MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Motion for summary adjudication as to the cause of action for conversion is denied as moot; dismissal of that cause of action on file. Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is framed by the pleadings. Plaintiff concedes its burden to plead and prove an existence of a contract. Operative complaint only alleges an oral partnership agreement formed in 2001 by Plaintiff, Blake Scholten and James Scholten, and as original trustor (no allegation in the complaint as to the ownership of the property at the time of the agreement or if Mr. Scholten was also the sole original trustee), under which James Scholten stopped performing in 2002; the agreement was later amended as described in paragraph 8 (the Court will note that no deadlines for performance under the contract, including transfer of interest or payment is alleged) and the only parties to that amended agreement were Plaintiff and james Scholten. There are no allegations in the complaint showing how Helen Scholten in her capacity as a trustee or individually became a party to this contract. Opposition to the motion for summary judgment suggests that Plaintiff may be asserting some kind of ratification, succession (either as individual or as trustee) or formation of a separate agreement with Helen Scholten by conduct (either as individual or as trustee). Therefore, Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege facts showing that Helen Scholten, as individual or trustee, is a party to the 2004 amended partnership agreement as described in paragraph 8 of the complaint or some other partnership agreement formed between 2004 and 2017. Cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty depends on the existence of the partnership agreement to which Helen Scholten is a party and Plaintiff shall have leave to include this cause of action in the amended complaint only if Plaintiff can in good faith allege facts to establish existence of the agreement. The Court does not reach the issue of the statute of limitations at this time. Cause of action for Interference with a contract is predicated on existence of a valid contract. Plaintiff alleges a conclusion that a valid contract existed. Plaintiff does not allege facts showing that Plaintiff had a right under recorded title or agreement (e.g. lease, partnership agreement, management agreement, employment agreement) to occupy and rent the premises to third parties. Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege such facts, how and when title was recorded; how and when the agreement was formed (note paragraph 8 of complaint does not allege right to possess and rent independent from agreement to transfer ownership interest); who were the parties to the agreement, what were the terms of the agreement, etc. Trial date is vacated. Case is re-set to CMC calendar. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: THREE EMBARCADERO CENTER VENTURE, A CALIFORNIA VS. THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Twelve Affirmative Defenses Alleged In Answer To First Amended Complaint And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 5, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF THREE EMBARCADERO CENTER VENTURE, A CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Twelve Affirmative Defenses Alleged In Answer To First Amended Complaint And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact including, but not limited to facts 7-18, incorporated by reference into every issue presented for adjudication. Motions for summary adjudication is denied for an additional reason - failure to comply with CRC 3.1350(b), compare issues to be adjudicated as stated in the notice of motion and in the separate statement. Prevailing party to prepare order in accordance with CCP 437c(g). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 281 ELLIS STREET LLC VS. WATCHFUL INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Watchful, Inc.S Cross-Complaint And For Summary Adjudication As To Watchful, Inc.S Second Affirmative Defense
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 5, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFF 281 ELLIS STREET LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Watchful, Inc.S Cross-Complaint And For Summary Adjudication As To Watchful, Inc.S Second Affirmative Defense is continued to September 3, 2021 to be heard with other pending motion. The Court notes that on March 2, 2021 parties filed a joint Stipulation to Alternative Dispute Resolution. Parties failed to respond to two e-mails sent by the case management department (3/11/21 and 5/21/21). No later than August 10, 2021 parties to respond to those e-mail communications and in good faith cooperate on setting a date for judicial mediation. No later than August 13, 2021 each party to submit compliance declaration. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
